##NAMES=Regerite
##RRUFFID=R250087
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KFe_6_(PO_4_)_4_(OH)_7_(H_2_O)_6_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Kreuzberg pegmatite, Pleystein, Oberpfalz, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Clusters of yellowish-green prisms found in vugs on a specimen of drusy quartz, associated with rockbridgeite, strengite and phosphosiderite. Type sample, described in Rewitzer et al (2023)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250087
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=K_0.95_(Fe^3+^_5.66_Ti_0.45_)(PO_4_)_3.95_(OH)_7_[(H_2_O)_5.33_(OH)_0.88_]·4H_2_O ; 
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 15.408 b: 17.311 c: 9.87 alpha: 90 beta: 95.42 gamma: 90 volume: 2621 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
